display.purpose: The hero headline only (.hero-title). The one place the system goes maximally loud.

headline.purpose: Every section h2 — scales from 36px to 48px at md, 60px for the climactic contact-section headline.

body.purpose: Reading copy. 16px is the page's typographic floor; 18px is reserved for the one substantial About paragraph.

label.purpose: System chrome: nav links, buttons, the language toggle, eyebrows, and section-index numerals (02 / 03 / 04).

shadows.name: focal-glow
shadows.purpose: Tight halo confirming "this is alive/active" — the brand dot and the active nav-link underline.

shadows.name: button-glow
shadows.purpose: Resting elevation for the primary button. Intensifies to 0 14px 38px rgba(255,106,26,.4) on hover — the only elevation state that changes on interaction.

shadows.name: structural-float
shadows.purpose: Glass-panel and the floating nav bar: a soft inset highlight plus a large ambient drop, plus a whisper of orange glow at the same radius.

shadows.name: cursor-halo
shadows.purpose: The custom cursor's signature double-ring glow.

shadows.name: orbit-core-glow
shadows.purpose: The single brightest point on the page, marking the orbit diagram's center.

shadows.name: ember-glow
shadows.purpose: The ambient floating particles drifting up from the lower viewport.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing for nav-link underlines, buttons, the nav-cta, and every scroll-reveal transition.

motion.name: reveal-duration
motion.purpose: Duration for the fade + rise + blur-clear scroll-reveal pattern (.reveal-on-scroll / .animate-fade-up).

motion.name: press-feedback
motion.purpose: Identical :active press feedback across every interactive element — buttons, nav-cta, social pills.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The loudest action on any given screen. Used for the hero and contact CTAs.
components.html: Contact on WhatsApp
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #ff8a3d, #ff6a1a); color: #ffffff; font-family: 'Geist Mono', monospace; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; line-height: 1.4; padding: 12px 32px; border-radius: 9999px; border: none; box-shadow: 0 12px 30px rgba(255,106,26,.28); transition: box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1), transform 160ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover, .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { box-shadow: 0 14px 38px rgba(255,106,26,.4); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-icon { width: 16px; height: 16px; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary action. Never gains a glow on hover — glow stays reserved for the primary variant.
components.html: Let's Talk
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: transparent; color: #ffffff; font-family: 'Geist Mono', monospace; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; line-height: 1.4; padding: 12px 32px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,150,86,.3); transition: background-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1), border-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1), transform 160ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover, .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { background: rgba(255,138,61,.08); border-color: rgba(255,150,86,.55); } .ds-btn-outline:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-icon { width: 16px; height: 16px; }

components.name: Social Pill
components.description: Identity marks for third-party links (WhatsApp, LinkedIn, Behance) — deliberately skip the Geist Mono / uppercase chrome treatment since they're brand names, not UI labels.
components.html: WhatsApp
components.css: .ds-social-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 12px 24px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.1); background: rgba(255,255,255,.05); color: #f1ece4; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; text-decoration: none; transition: background-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); } .ds-social-pill:hover, .ds-social-pill:focus-visible { background: rgba(255,255,255,.1); } .ds-social-pill:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-pill-icon { width: 16px; height: 16px; }

components.name: Glass Card
components.description: The base surface for orbit-diagram cards and stat callouts: smoked glass over ambient glow, never opaque.
components.css: .ds-card-glass { background: linear-gradient(145deg, rgba(28,20,11,.62), rgba(8,6,3,.42)); border: 1px solid rgba(255,150,86,.14); border-radius: 1.5rem; padding: 16px; box-shadow: inset 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,.05), 0 30px 80px rgba(0,0,0,.35), 0 0 30px rgba(255,106,26,.05); backdrop-filter: blur(20px); } .ds-card-eyebrow { font-family: 'Geist Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.16em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #cabba9; margin: 0 0 6px; } .ds-card-body { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 16px; color: #f1ece4; margin: 0; }

components.name: Floating Nav
components.description: A floating glass capsule inset from the viewport edge. Gains a darker, more opaque background once the page scrolls past the hero.
components.html: SİMAY ERNALBANTAboutLet's Talk
components.css: .ds-topnav { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 28px; padding: 0 24px; height: 64px; border-radius: 18px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,138,61,.2); background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(20,15,9,.78), rgba(8,6,3,.62)); box-shadow: inset 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,.06), 0 15px 55px rgba(0,0,0,.55), 0 0 24px rgba(255,106,26,.08); } .ds-brand { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; font-family: 'Sora', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 16px; letter-spacing: 0.06em; color: #f4ece2; text-decoration: none; } .ds-brand-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: #ff6a1a; box-shadow: 0 0 10px #ff6a1a; } .ds-nav-link { position: relative; font-family: 'Geist Mono', monospace; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #fff; text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-link::after { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 0; bottom: -4px; height: 1px; width: 0; background: #ff8a3d; box-shadow: 0 0 10px #ff8a3d; transition: width 250ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); } .ds-nav-link:hover::after, .ds-nav-active::after { width: 100%; } .ds-nav-active { color: #ff8a3d; } .ds-nav-cta { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; margin-left: auto; padding: 8px 20px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,150,86,.35); color: #fff; font-family: 'Geist Mono', monospace; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; text-decoration: none; transition: background-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); } .ds-nav-cta:hover, .ds-nav-cta:focus-visible { background: rgba(255,138,61,.1); } .ds-nav-cta:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-icon { width: 14px; height: 14px; }

components.name: Orbit Core
components.description: Signature component: the glowing core of the Education & Past orbit diagram — the single brightest point on the page, geometry standing in for decoration.
components.css: .ds-orbit-wrap { position: relative; width: 240px; height: 240px; } .ds-orbit-ring { position: absolute; top: 50%; left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -50%); border: 1px solid rgba(255,150,86,.12); border-radius: 50%; } .ds-orbit-core { position: absolute; top: 50%; left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -50%); width: 14%; height: 14%; border-radius: 50%; background: radial-gradient(circle, #ffb066, #ff6a1a 55%, transparent 80%); filter: blur(2px); box-shadow: 0 0 60px 20px rgba(255,106,26,.35); }

narrative.overview:
This system reads like a colorist's final grade on a film: every glow, blend mode, and motion timing is a deliberate, exact choice, never an accident or a default left untouched. The portfolio's job is to convert a skeptical hiring lead into outreach within a few seconds of scrolling, so the page itself has to perform as evidence of craft — the same precision that compiles a brand identity or interface also compiles this site's mix-blend-mode video compositing, its orbit-diagram geometry, and its scroll-reveal timing. Drama comes from light and motion concentrated on a small number of focal elements (the cursor halo, the hero video, the orbit core), never from decoration spread evenly across the page.

The palette is deliberately monochromatic at the hue level: one combustion-orange family against a near-black carbon base, because restraint is the confidence signal this brand is built on. The system explicitly rejects the generic AI-portfolio toolkit — tiny uppercase eyebrows stacked on every section, gradient-filled headline text, glassmorphism used as a default rather than a deliberate accent, identical icon-card grids, and the "hero metric" template (oversized stat + label, repeated three times). Where this system uses a glass surface, a glow, or an uppercase label, it is because that specific element earns it, not because the template calls for it everywhere.
keyCharacteristics: One accent hue (combustion orange), expressed at multiple intensities, never joined by a second hue.
Near-black, warm-tinted carbon base — never a cool gray or pure #000.
Glow stands in for shadow as the entire elevation language.
Three typefaces, each locked to one role: display/headline structure, reading copy, or mono "chrome" (labels, numerals, nav).
Motion is restrained and directional (fade + rise + blur-clear), not bouncy or playful.

rules.name: The Single-Accent Rule
rules.body: Exactly one hue family (combustion orange) carries the system. If a UI element needs a second color to read correctly, the fix is a different orange intensity or a neutral, never a new hue.

rules.name: The Ambient-Glow Rule
rules.body: glow-orange (#FF6A1A) and glow-amber (#E8590C) are reserved for atmosphere, not interface: the two fixed .ambient-glow blobs (blurred 150px, 14% opacity) and the .ember particles. They never appear on a clickable element or a text color — that's primary / primary-container's job. Keeping atmosphere and interaction on separate token pairs is what stops the glow from reading as noise.

rules.name: The Three-Voice Rule
rules.body: Sora speaks only for display/headline structure, Inter only for reading copy, Geist Mono only for chrome. None of the three ever covers for another — if a piece of UI needs a label treatment, it gets Geist Mono and uppercase tracking, not a small Sora heading.

rules.name: The Glow-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Depth comes from a colored or white glow at an element's edge, never a plain dark drop shadow. If a new component needs to feel "raised," reach for a glow token above before reaching for box-shadow: 0 4px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.X).

dos: Do keep every new accent color inside the Combustion Orange family (primary #ff8a3d / primary-container #ff6a1a) at a different lightness or opacity rather than introducing a new hue.
Do use a glow (colored or white, per the Elevation shadow vocabulary) as the only depth cue for anything meant to feel "raised" or "alive."
Do keep the three-typeface role split absolute: Sora for display/headline, Inter for reading copy, Geist Mono uppercase for all chrome (nav, labels, numerals, buttons).

Do add a prefers-reduced-motion: reduce alternative for every opacity/transform/blur-based reveal and for the .ember particle animation. PRODUCT.md commits this project to WCAG 2.1 AA, which requires it, and index.html currently has zero prefers-reduced-motion handling anywhere — this is an open gap, not a style choice.

Do treat the numbered section markers (02 / 03 / 04) as acceptable, per PRODUCT.md's Anti-references: they trace a real, ordered path through the portfolio (Expertise → Web → Education), not decorative scaffolding.
donts: Don't add a tiny uppercase eyebrow to every section by reflex — PRODUCT.md names this directly as a generic AI-template tell. Use one only where it does real wayfinding work (as the existing eyebrows already do).

Don't fill headline text with a gradient (background-clip: text). The system's existing technique for a "hot" headline word — solid Bone White or Combustion Orange plus a text-shadow glow (.text-glow-orange, .text-glow) — is the correct, already-established way to add drama to type.

Don't make glassmorphism (backdrop-filter: blur) the default surface treatment for every container. It's reserved for glass-panel contexts (cards floating over video/ambient glow); a plain surface-container flat fill is correct everywhere else.
Don't build identical icon-card grids or repeat the "hero metric" template (oversized stat + label, ×3). PRODUCT.md calls both out by name as anti-references for this brand.
Don't introduce a second shadow vocabulary (standard gray Material elevation steps). Every "raised" surface in this system goes through the Glow-Not-Shadow Rule instead.

Don't wire up the dead display-2xl / display-xl / headline-lg / headline-lg-mobile Tailwind tokens without also auditing whether .hero-title and the text-4xl/md:text-5xl pattern should be replaced by them — right now both systems exist and only one is real; adding a third sizing source would make it worse, not better.
